title: rosa communis var. viridicata
intro: Rosa communis var. viridicata, also known as the green briar or wood rose, is a perennial plant in the rose family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant's leaves and stems have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Rosa communis var. viridicata include treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating digestive issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, although more research is needed to fully confirm its effectiveness and safety for human use.
